heading: Standards for safety and soundness
label: 1831p–1 Standards for safety and soundness

children.label: A
children.text: internal controls, information systems, and internal audit systems, in accordance with ;section 1831m of this title

children.label: B
children.text: loan documentation;

children.label: C
children.text: credit underwriting;

children.label: D
children.text: interest rate exposure;

children.label: E
children.text: asset growth; and

children.label: F
children.text: compensation, fees, and benefits, in accordance with subsection (c); and
such other operational and managerial standards as the agency determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Asset quality, earnings, and stock valuation standards
children.label: b Asset quality, earnings, and stock valuation standards
children.text: Each appropriate Federal banking agency shall prescribe standards, by regulation or guideline, for all insured depository institutions relating to asset quality, earnings, and stock valuation that the agency determines to be appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: would provide any executive officer, employee, director, or principal shareholder of the institution with excessive compensation, fees or benefits; or

children.label: B
children.text: could lead to material financial loss to the institution;

children.label: A
children.text: the combined value of all cash and noncash benefits provided to the individual;

children.label: B
children.text: the compensation history of the individual and other individuals with comparable expertise at the institution;

children.label: C
children.text: the financial condition of the institution;

children.label: D
children.text: comparable compensation practices at comparable institutions, based upon such factors as asset size, geographic location, and the complexity of the loan portfolio or other assets;

children.label: E
children.text: for postemployment benefits, the projected total cost and benefit to the institution;

children.label: F
children.text: any connection between the individual and any fraudulent act or omission, breach of trust or fiduciary duty, or insider abuse with regard to the institution; and

children.label: G
children.text: other factors that the agency determines to be relevant; and
such other standards relating to compensation, fees, and benefits as the agency determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Standards under subsections (a), (b), and (c) shall be prescribed by regulation or guideline. Such regulations or guidelines may not prescribe standards that set a specific level or range of compensation for directors, officers, or employees of insured depository institutions.

children.heading: Applicability of other laws
children.label: 2 Applicability of other laws
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not affect the authority of any appropriate Federal banking agency to restrict the level of compensation, including golden parachute payments (as defined in ), paid to any director, officer, or employee of an insured depository institution under any other provision of law.section 1828(k)(4) of this title

children.heading: Senior executive officers at undercapitalized institutions
children.label: 3 Senior executive officers at undercapitalized institutions
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not affect the authority of any appropriate Federal banking agency to restrict compensation paid to any senior executive officer of an undercapitalized insured depository institution pursuant to section 1831 of this title.o

children.heading: Safety and soundness or enforcement actions
children.label: 4 Safety and soundness or enforcement actions
A
children.text: to preserve the safety and soundness of the institution; or

children.label: B
children.text: in connection with any action under or any order issued by the agency, any agreement between the agency and the institution, or any condition imposed by the agency in connection with the agency’s approval of an application or other request by the institution, which is enforceable under .section 1818 of this titlesection 1818 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: if such standard is prescribed by regulation of the agency, the agency shall require the institution to submit an acceptable plan to the agency within the time allowed by the agency under subparagraph (C); and

children.label: ii
children.text: if such standard is prescribed by guideline, the agency may require the institution to submit a plan described in clause (i).

children.heading: Contents of plan
children.label: B Contents of plan
children.text: Any plan required under subparagraph (A) shall specify the steps that the institution will take to correct the deficiency. If the institution is undercapitalized, the plan may be part of a capital restoration plan.

children.heading: Deadlines for submission and review of plans
children.label: C Deadlines for submission and review of plans
i
children.text: provide institutions with reasonable time to submit plans required under subparagraph (A), and generally require the institution to submit a plan not later than 30 days after the agency determines that the institution fails to meet any standard prescribed under subsection (a), (b), or (c); and

children.label: ii
children.text: require the agency to act on plans expeditiously, and generally not later than 30 days after the plan is submitted.

children.heading: Order required if institution fails to submit or implement plan
children.label: 2 Order required if institution fails to submit or implement plan
A
children.text: shall require the institution to correct the deficiency; and

children.label: i
children.text: Prohibit the institution from permitting its average total assets during any calendar quarter to exceed its average total assets during the preceding calendar quarter, or restrict the rate at which the average total assets of the institution may increase from one calendar quarter to another.

children.label: ii
children.text: Require the institution to increase its ratio of tangible equity to assets.

children.label: iii
children.text: Take the action described in section 1831(f)(2)(C) of this title.o

children.label: iv
children.text: Require the institution to take any other action that the agency determines will better carry out the purpose of section 1831 of this title than any of the actions described in this subparagraph.o

children.label: A
children.text: the agency determines that the insured depository institution fails to meet any standard prescribed under subsection (a)(1) or (b)(1);

children.label: B
children.text: the institution has not corrected the deficiency; and

children.label: I
children.text: the institution commenced operations; or

children.label: II
children.text: 1 or more persons acquired control of the institution; or

children.label: ii
children.text: during the 18-month period before the date on which the institution first failed to meet the standard, the institution underwent extraordinary growth, as defined by the agency.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: f Definitions
children.text: For purposes of this section, the terms “average” and “capital restoration plan” have the same meanings as in section 1831 of this title.o

children.heading: Other authority not affected
children.label: g Other authority not affected
children.text: The authority granted by this section is in addition to any other authority of the Federal banking agencies.
